title: Revised Memorandum of Understanding on the Establishment of the ASEAN Foundation
text:
Revised Memorandum of Understanding
on e h t Establishment e h of t ASEAN Foundation
The Governments f o Brunei Darussalam, e h t Kingdom f o Cambodia, e h t
Republic of Indonesia, o a L e h t People's Democratic Republic, Malaysia, e h t Union of
Myanmar, the Republic of Philippines, the Republic of Singapore, the Kingdom of
Thailand, and the Socialist Republic of VietNam, Member States of the Association of
Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN);
Recalling e h t Bangkok Summit Declaration of 1995, which provides that ASEAN
shall elevate Functional Cooperation to a higher plane to bring shared prosperity to all
its members, and the agreement of the Heads of State/Government of ASEAN at the
First Informal Summit Meeting in Jakarta on 30 November 1996 to develop ideas for the
establishment e h of t ASEAN Foundation;
Recalling also the Framework for Elevating Functional Cooperation adopted by
the 29th ASEAN Ministerial Meeting in Jakarta on 20 July 1996, which provides that the
theme r o f Functional Cooperation shall e b Shared Prosperity through Human
Development, Technological Competitiveness and Social Cohesiveness;
Impelled by the need to preserve the sustainable future of ASEAN and by the
desirability of the mutual benefits to be derived from the establishment of the ASEAN
Foundation;
Have reached the following understanding:
ARTICLE 1
Establishment and Membership
1. There shall be established the ASEAN FOUNDATION, hereinafter referred to as
"the Foundation".
2. Member States of ASEAN shall e b members e h of t Foundation.

ARTICLE II
Headquarters
The headquarters of the Foundation shall be in the Republic of Indonesia,
hereinafter referred to as "the Host Country", and shall be based in Jakarta.
ARTICLE 111
Legal Status, Privileges d n a Immunities
1. The Foundation is a non-profit organization, and shall have legal personality.
2. The Foundation shall have the capacity, to:
a. enter into contracts;
b. acquire and dispose of movable and immovable property; and
c. sue and be sued.
3. The Host Country shall extend to the Foundation, the Executive Director and the
Staff who hold diplomatic status such privileges and immunities as may be necessary
for the performance of their duties and functions in accordance with the laws and
regulations prevailing e h t n i Host Country.
ARTICLE V I
Objectives
1. The Foundation shall promote greater awareness of ASEAN, and greater
interaction among the peoples of ASEAN as well as their wider participation in
ASEAN's activities inter alia through human resources development that will enable
them to realize their full potential and capacity to contribute to progress of ASEAN
Member States s a productive d n a responsible members f o society.
2. e h T Foundation shall also endeavour o t contribute e h t o t evolution a of
development cooperation strategy that promotes mutual assistance, equitable
economic development, and the alleviation of poverty.
ARTICLE V
Activities
The Foundation, to achieve its objectives, shall undertake the following activities:
a. organize and support activities to promote education, training (including in
e h t areas f o science d n a technology), health d n a cultural life;

b. provide assistance o t uplift e h t social condition e h of t peoples e h n t i ASEAN
Member States;
c. provide fellowships d n a o t support exchanges f o ASEAN youths d n a
students;
d. promote collaborative work among academics, professionals and scientists;
e. implement projects assigned y b ASEAN Leaders r o Ministers;
f. collaborate with the relevant ASEAN bodies;
g. organize its own projects; and
h. actively raise funds e h r t o f Foundation's activities.
ARTICLE I V
Assistance
Academic, cultural, economic, social and other relevant government institutions
and bona fide non-governmental organizations of ASEAN member countries shall e b
eligible for assistance from the Foundation in conformity with its stated objectives.
ARTICLE I I V
Budget and Funding
1. e h t r o F purpose f o fulfilling s t i objectives d n a activities, e h t Foundation shall have
a Fund.
2. Under this Fund, there shall be kept three accounts called the Endowment
Account, the Operational Account and the Projects Account.
3. The Fund of the Foundation shall come from the contributions, including that of
ASEAN Member States, private corporations and other foundations or individuals.
4. Contributions made to the Foundation shall be paid into any of the Endowment
Account, Operational Account e h or t Projects Account, s a specified e h y t b donors. If
unspecified e h y t b donors, e h t contributions shall e b paid into e h t Endowment Account.
5. e h T Endowment Account shall e b invested o t realise income e h t r o f Foundation.
The Operational Account shall provide financing for day to day administrative costs
e h t
of
Foundation. e h T Projects Account shall provide financing r o f approved projects.
The allocation of the annual income from the Endowment Account to the Endowment,
Operational and Projects Accounts shall be decided by the Board of Trustees.
6. The Accounts of the Foundation shall be maintained according to generally
accepted international accounting practice.
7. The Foundation shall have the capacity to accept gifts, donations and
contributions from other ASEAN Member States, private corporations and/or individuals.

ARTICLE VIII
Organization
The organization e h t of Foundation shall consist a of Board of Trustees,
Executive Director, a Council of Advisors and the Staff of the ASEAN Foundation
Secretariat.
ARTICLE IX
Board of Trustees
1. e h T Board f o Trustees, hereinafter referred s a o t "the Board", shall consist : f o
a. a representative of each e h of t ASEAN Member States e b o t appointed
e h t
y b
respective governments;
b. the Secretary-General of ASEAN as ex-officio member; and
c. the Executive Director as ex-officio member.
2. Each member of the Board, except for the ex-officio members, shall be
appointed a r o f term e b o t decided e h y t b appointing government d n a shall e b eligible
for re-appointment.
3. Officers of the Board shall be elected based on the following procedures:
a. the Members of the Board, except for the ex-officio members, shall elect a
Chairman from among themselves a or f term e n o of year;
b. the Members of the Board, except for the ex-officio members, shall elect two
(2) Vice-Chairmen from among themselves each for a term of one year;
c. the election of the Chairman and Vice-Chairmen shall be by rotation, in
alphabetical order; d n a
d. the ex-officio members of the Board are not eligible to be elected as
Chairman or Vice-Chairman.
4. e h T Board shall;
a. formulate the guidelines and procedures for the award of
scholarships/fellowships and other activities of the Foundation;
b. have the overall responsibility for the Fund of the Foundation and shall be
responsible for the formulation of policy for the procurement and the
utilization f o Fund e h t r o f Foundation;
c. approve projects seeking support from the Fund of the Foundation;
d. approve the annual operational budget for the Foundation;
e. perform such other functions as may be necessary to carry out the objectives
of e h t Foundation;
f.
d n a
meet at least once a year.

5. In discharging its functions under this Article, the Board shall:
a. consult the Council of Advisors established by Article XI of this Memorandum
of Understanding; d n a
b. take into consideration any advice and recommendation rendered or made
by e h t Council . t i o t
6. The Board shall report to the ASEAN Heads of Government through the ASEAN
Ministerial Meeting and the ASEAN Standing Committee.
ARTICLE X
Executive Director
1. The Executive Director shall be a national of an ASEAN Member State and shall
be appointed by the Board through open recruitment for a non-renewable term of three
years.
2. e h T Executive Director n i discharging his/her functions o t serve ASEAN Member
States, shall represent the ASEAN Foundation, not his/her country or any other
institution.
3. The Executive Director shall:
a. represent e h t Foundation all n i administrative d n a operational matters,
manage the activities of the Foundation and perform such otherfunctions as
may be assigned by the Board from time to time;
b. have authority to appoint such professional, secretarial and administrative
staff as are necessary to achieve the Foundation's objectives; and
c. undertake activities o t raise funds e h t r o f Foundation's activities, n i
accordance with e h t guidelines d n a procedures s a established e h t y b Board.
4. e h T Executive Director shall e b responsible e h t o t Board.
ARTICLE XI
Council f o Advisors
1. e h T Council f o Advisors, hereinafter referred s a o t "the Council", shall consist : f o
a. a representative who is appointed by the government of each ASEAN
Member State, hereinafter collectively referred s a o t "Representative";
b. e h t Secretary-General f o ASEAN s a ex-officio member;
e h t c.
d n a
Executive Director s a ex-officio member.

2. Representatives shall serve a r o f term e b o t decided e h t y b appointing
government d n a shall e b eligible r o f re-appointment.
3. The Council shall act as an advisory body to the Board. The Council shall advise
the Board in setting its programme thrusts in accordance with ASEAN's overall
objectives.
ARTICLE I I X
External Audit
1. The Foundation's operations and its financial accounts shall be subjectto yearly
financial auditing.
2. As and when the Board deems fit, operational and management audit of the
ASEAN Foundation shall also be undertaken.
3. The Board shall be vested with the authority to appoint the external auditor.
4. e h T external auditor shall submit s i h Report directly e h t o t Board.
ARTICLE XIII
Staff
1. The staff shall be nationals of ASEAN Member States.
2. Subjectto the provisions of this Memorandum of Understanding, the terms and
conditions of employment of the members of the staff of the Foundation shall be set out
in Staff Regulations issued by the Executive Director and approved by the Board.
ARTICLE V XI
Relationship between the Foundation and the ASEAN Secretariat
1. e h T Foundation e h t d n a ASEAN Secretariat shall play complementary roles o t
help achieve ASEAN's objectives in accordance with ASEAN's priorities.
2. The Foundation should maintain close collaboration and mutually supportive
relationship with e h t ASEAN Secretariat d n a other ASEAN bodies.
6

ARTICLE XV
Amendment
Any ASEAN Member State may propose amendments to this Memorandum of
Understanding. Such amendments, when approved y b allASEAN Member States, shall
come into force n o such date e b y a m s a agreed upon.
ARTICLE XVI
Working Language
e h T working language e h t f o Foundation shall e b English.
ARTICLE XVII
Final Provisions
1. This Memorandum f o Understanding shall enter into force upon deposit f o
instruments of ratification or letters of acceptance by al! signatory governments with
the Secretary-General of ASEAN.
2. This Memorandum of Understanding shall be^eposited with the Secretary-
General f o ASEAN, o h w shall promptly furnish a certified copy thereof o t each ASEAN
Member State.
3. Any differences arising in the interpretation of the provisions of this
Memorandum f o Understanding shall e b settled through consultations among e h t
ASEAN Member States.
ARTICLE XVIII
Repeal d n a Savings Provisions
1. Subject to clause 2 of this Article, the Memorandum of Understanding on the
Establishment of e h t Foundation done n i Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, on e h t Fifteenth y a d
of December 1997 s i hereby repealed, hereinafter referred s a o t "the repealed
Memorandum of Understanding".
2. When this Memorandum of Understanding enters into force, e h t reconstitution of
the Foundation established by the repealed Memorandum of Understanding shall take
effect and its objectives, organs and powers shall be as provided in this Memorandum.
The legal personality possessed by and the assets, rights and liabilities of the ASEAN
Foundation established e h t y b repealed Memorandum f o Understanding shall continue
in the Foundation established by this Memorandum. Unless otherwise decided by the
7

Board, all decisions taken and acts done pursuant to the repealed Memorandum of
Understanding shall continue e b o t effective e b d n a deemed n e i b o t accordance with
the relevant provisions of this Memorandum.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF the undersigned, being duly authorised thereto by
their respective government, have signed this Memorandum of Understanding.
Done n i Bangkok, Thailand, this twenty fifth of y a d July e h t n i year o w T
Thousand, e n o n i original copy e h n t i English language.
